root_text.ud8.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati pubbārāme migāramātupāsāde.
root_text.ud8.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena visākhāya migāramātuyā nattā kālaṅkatā hoti piyā manāpā.
root_text.ud8.8:1.4: Atha kho visākhā migāramātā allavatthā allakesā divā divassa yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud8.8:1.5: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho visākhaṁ migāramātaraṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud8.8:2.1: “Handa kuto nu tvaṁ, visākhe, āgacchasi allavatthā allakesā idhūpasaṅkantā divā divassā”ti?
root_text.ud8.8:2.3: Tenāhaṁ allavatthā allakesā idhūpasaṅkantā divā divassā”ti.
root_text.ud8.8:2.4: “Iccheyyāsi tvaṁ, visākhe, yāvatikā sāvatthiyā manussā tāvatike putte ca nattāro cā”ti?
root_text.ud8.8:2.5: “Iccheyyāhaṁ, bhagavā, yāvatikā sāvatthiyā manussā tāvatike putte ca nattāro cā”ti.
root_text.ud8.8:3.1: “Kīvabahukā pana, visākhe, sāvatthiyā manussā devasikaṁ kālaṁ karontī”ti?
root_text.ud8.8:3.2: “Dasapi, bhante, sāvatthiyā manussā devasikaṁ kālaṁ karonti;
root_text.ud8.8:3.10: dvepi, bhante, sāvatthiyā manussā devasikaṁ kālaṁ karonti.
root_text.ud8.8:3.11: Ekopi, bhante, sāvatthiyā manusso devasikaṁ kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.ud8.8:3.12: Avivittā, bhante, sāvatthi manussehi kālaṁ karontehī”ti.
root_text.ud8.8:4.1: “Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, visākhe, api nu tvaṁ kadāci karahaci anallavatthā vā bhaveyyāsi anallakesā vā”ti?
root_text.ud8.8:4.3: Alaṁ me, bhante, tāva bahukehi puttehi ca nattārehi cā”ti.
root_text.ud8.8:5.1: “Yesaṁ kho, visākhe, sataṁ piyāni, sataṁ tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.2: yesaṁ navuti piyāni, navuti tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.3: yesaṁ asīti piyāni, asīti tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.4: yesaṁ sattati piyāni, sattati tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.5: yesaṁ saṭṭhi piyāni, saṭṭhi tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.6: yesaṁ paññāsaṁ piyāni, paññāsaṁ tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.7: yesaṁ cattārīsaṁ piyāni, cattārīsaṁ tesaṁ dukkhāni,
root_text.ud8.8:5.8: yesaṁ tiṁsaṁ piyāni, tiṁsaṁ tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.9: yesaṁ vīsati piyāni, vīsati tesaṁ dukkhāni,
root_text.ud8.8:5.10: yesaṁ dasa piyāni, dasa tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.11: yesaṁ nava piyāni, nava tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.12: yesaṁ aṭṭha piyāni, aṭṭha tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.13: yesaṁ satta piyāni, satta tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.14: yesaṁ cha piyāni, cha tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.15: yesaṁ pañca piyāni, pañca tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.16: yesaṁ cattāri piyāni, cattāri tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.17: yesaṁ tīṇi piyāni, tīṇi tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.18: yesaṁ dve piyāni, dve tesaṁ dukkhāni;
root_text.ud8.8:5.19: yesaṁ ekaṁ piyaṁ, ekaṁ tesaṁ dukkhaṁ;
root_text.ud8.8:5.20: yesaṁ natthi piyaṁ, natthi tesaṁ dukkhaṁ, asokā te virajā anupāyāsāti vadāmī”ti.
root_text.ud8.8:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud8.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the stilt longhouse of Migāra’s mother in the Eastern Monastery.
translation_text.ud8.8:1.3: Now at that time the dear and beloved granddaughter of Visākhā Migāra’s Mother had just passed away.
translation_text.ud8.8:1.4: Then, in the middle of the day, Visākhā with wet clothes and hair went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down.
translation_text.ud8.8:2.1: “So, Visākhā, where are you coming from in the middle of the day with wet clothes and hair?”
translation_text.ud8.8:2.2: “Sir, my beloved granddaughter has just passed away.
translation_text.ud8.8:2.3: That’s why I came here in the middle of the day with wet clothes and hair.”
translation_text.ud8.8:2.4: “Visākhā, would you like as many children and grandchildren as there are people in the whole of Sāvatthī?”
translation_text.ud8.8:3.1: “But Visākhā, how many people pass away each day in Sāvatthī?”
translation_text.ud8.8:3.2: “Every day, sir, there are ten people passing away in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.ud8.8:3.11: or at least one person who passes away every day in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.ud8.8:3.12: Sāvatthī is never without someone passing away.”
translation_text.ud8.8:4.1: “What do you think, Visākhā? Would there ever be a time when your clothes and hair were not wet?”
translation_text.ud8.8:4.3: Enough, sir, with so many children and grandchildren.”
translation_text.ud8.8:5.1: “Those who have a hundred loved ones, Visākhā, have a hundred sufferings.
translation_text.ud8.8:5.2: Those who have ninety loved ones,
translation_text.ud8.8:5.19: or one loved one have one suffering.
translation_text.ud8.8:5.20: Those who have no loved ones have no suffering. They are free of sorrow, stains, and anguish I say.”
translation_text.ud8.8:6.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud8.8:7.2: and the countless forms of suffering in the world
translation_text.ud8.8:7.3: occur because of those that we love;
translation_text.ud8.8:7.4: without loved ones they do not occur.
translation_text.ud8.8:8.1: That’s why those who have no loved ones <j>at all in the world
translation_text.ud8.8:8.2: are happy and free of grief.
translation_text.ud8.8:8.3: So aspiring to the sorrowless and stainless,
translation_text.ud8.8:8.4: have no loved ones in the world at all.”
